construct_description: Calibrate how much AI involvement is appropriate for each task given stakes, accountability, and context.
context: You are a Financial Analyst at Westbrook Capital Advisors. Work through your task queue for the day — each item requires you to decide how much AI involvement is appropriate.

tasks.title: Annual Report Executive Brief
tasks.briefing: Your managing director needs a one-page executive brief distilling the key metrics from an 80-page annual report for a target acquisition company. She needs it before her 3pm call with the investment committee — key financials, growth metrics, risk flags.
tasks.stakes_reason: Factual synthesis of public financial data — the MD will review and draw her own conclusions before any decisions are made.
tasks.correct_ai_reason: Extracting and summarizing key metrics from a structured financial document is high-value AI work. Verify the numbers against the source document, but don't manually compile what AI can handle in seconds.
tasks.ai_draft: Executive Brief — [Company] Annual Report: Revenue: $847M (+14% YoY). Gross Margin: 42% (up from 38%). EBITDA: $124M (+22%). Debt-to-Equity: 1.8x (elevated; monitor covenant compliance). Key growth driver: Enterprise segment +31%, offsetting flat SMB. Risk flags: Two material weakness disclosures in internal controls; pending litigation (IP dispute, $40M exposure). Cash position: $210M; FCF positive for third consecutive year. Analyst note: Strong topline growth with margin improvement, but internal control weaknesses warrant due diligence scrutiny.
tasks.teaching_point: Extracting and formatting key metrics from structured financial documents is prime AI territory. Your role is verification and judgment — not data entry.
tasks.writing_placeholder: Write the one-page executive brief summarizing the annual report's key metrics, growth indicators, and risk flags here...

tasks.title: Financial Plan for Divorce Client
tasks.briefing: Margaret Chen, a 52-year-old client going through a divorce, needs a personalized financial planning recommendation. She has $1.2M in marital assets, a pension, and a mortgage. She's asked you to recommend how to structure her post-divorce financial life, including asset division approach and retirement planning.
tasks.stakes_reason: A major life transition with significant financial consequences for a real person who trusts your judgment — requires your personal relationship and professional accountability.
tasks.correct_ai_reason: Personalized financial advice during a major life transition requires your professional judgment, your knowledge of Margaret's full situation, and your fiduciary accountability. AI cannot carry the relationship or the responsibility.
tasks.ai_draft: Financial Planning Framework — Divorce Transition: Asset Division Considerations: With $1.2M in marital assets, key factors include tax basis on investment accounts vs. retirement accounts, pension valuation (QDRO requirements), and home equity/mortgage assumption capacity. Recommend consulting a CDFA for formal asset division analysis. Retirement Planning: At 52 with a pension, focus on maximizing post-divorce contributions and adjusting beneficiary designations immediately. Cash Flow: Build 6-month emergency fund before making investment decisions. Immediate Actions: Update all beneficiary designations, establish individual credit history, review insurance coverage independently.
tasks.teaching_point: Fiduciary financial advice for clients in vulnerable life situations carries legal and ethical weight that belongs to you. The relationship, the accountability, and the personalized judgment are non-delegable.
tasks.writing_placeholder: Write your personalized financial planning recommendation for Margaret Chen here. Address her specific situation, priorities, and the key decisions she faces...

tasks.title: Budget Variance Analysis Template
tasks.briefing: The Operations team has been consistently overspending their budget by 15–20% for three consecutive quarters. Your director has asked you to create a budget variance analysis template the team can use monthly to track and explain variances by category.
tasks.stakes_reason: A useful tool that will be reviewed by finance staff before use — the format matters, but it will be customized and validated.
tasks.correct_ai_reason: Budget variance templates follow well-established financial formats AI handles confidently. You'll need to customize column structures and categories for the specific department, but AI gives you a strong starting framework.
tasks.ai_draft: Budget Variance Analysis Template — [Department/Month]: Categories: Personnel (Salaries, Benefits, Overtime, Contractors), Operating Expenses (Travel, Software/Licenses, Office Supplies, Professional Services), Capital Expenditures. For each category: Budget (Current Month), Actual (Current Month), Variance ($), Variance (%), YTD Budget, YTD Actual, YTD Variance (%), Explanation (required if >5% variance). Summary Row: Total Budget vs. Actual, Overall Variance %. Trend Section: 3-Month Variance Chart. Action Required: Categories with consecutive 2+ month unfavorable variances trigger mandatory remediation plan.
tasks.teaching_point: Financial templates with standard structures are strong AI-drafts candidates. Customize the category structure for the specific department's actual cost drivers before deploying.
tasks.writing_placeholder: Write or customize the budget variance analysis template here. Include all relevant categories and a structure that makes variance explanations easy to capture...

tasks.title: Loan Modification Request Decision
tasks.briefing: Robert Nguyen has been a client for 11 years and has missed three mortgage payments following a medical emergency. He's requesting a 6-month loan modification (reduced payment, interest accrual). Your approval or denial will be documented and potentially reviewed by compliance. Westbrook policy allows but does not require modifications in hardship cases.
tasks.stakes_reason: A discretionary decision affecting a long-standing client's housing stability, with documented accountability and compliance review.
tasks.correct_ai_reason: Loan modification decisions involve discretionary judgment about a real person's circumstances, documented accountability, and relationship factors that only you can weigh. The decision is yours.
tasks.ai_draft: Loan Modification Analysis — R. Nguyen: Account history shows 11 years of consistent payments with one prior hardship accommodation in 2019. Current arrears: $6,840 (3 payments). Medical hardship documentation: submitted and verified. Under proposed modification: reduced payment of $1,200/month for 6 months, deferred interest added to principal. Risk assessment: low re-default risk given long account history and documented temporary hardship. Comparable decisions in past 12 months: 7 of 9 similar requests approved under hardship policy.
tasks.teaching_point: Decisions with your name attached that affect a client's financial wellbeing require your judgment. AI can provide analysis, but the decision — and the accountability — is yours to own.
tasks.writing_placeholder: Write your decision and rationale for Robert Nguyen's loan modification request here. State your decision clearly and explain your reasoning...

tasks.title: Quarterly Client Portfolio Performance Letters
tasks.briefing: You need to draft the Q3 portfolio performance letters for your 40 clients. Each letter follows a standard format but is personalized with the client's name, account number, portfolio return, benchmark comparison, and a brief commentary on their specific allocation.
tasks.stakes_reason: Client-facing communications that are reviewed before sending — standard format with high-volume personalization.
tasks.correct_ai_reason: Quarterly performance letters follow a predictable format and are strong AI candidates — but you must verify the personalized data (returns, account numbers) and ensure the commentary accurately reflects each client's allocation.
tasks.ai_draft: Dear [Client Name], I hope this finds you well. Enclosed is your Q3 2024 portfolio performance summary for Account #[XXXX]. Your portfolio returned [X]% for the quarter, compared to the [benchmark] return of [Y]%. [Above/Below]-benchmark performance was driven primarily by your [equity/fixed income/alternative] allocation, which [outperformed/underperformed] as [brief market context]. Your portfolio remains positioned for [client objective — growth/income/preservation]. Looking ahead to Q4, [1-2 sentences on outlook relevant to their allocation]. Please don't hesitate to reach out with any questions. I look forward to our annual review in [month].
tasks.teaching_point: High-volume personalized communications with standard formats are ideal AI-drafts territory. Always verify the specific client data — returns, account numbers, allocation details — before sending.
tasks.writing_placeholder: Write or customize the quarterly portfolio performance letter template here. Include the standard sections and appropriate personalization placeholders...
